sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I have:
- Red: R, p, N
- Green: O, b, Y, M
For the palette order hypothesis, I need:
- Red: R, p, N ✓ (already correct!)
- Green: O, M, b, Y (need to reorder)
Current Green: [O, b, Y, M]
Target Green: [O, M, b, Y]
I need to swap b (green slot 2) with M (green slot 4), then swap Y (green slot 3) with whatever is now in slot 4.
Actually, let me just reorder the green box:
- Swap b (slot 2) ↔ M (slot 4): Green=[O, M, Y, b]
- Swap Y (slot 3) ↔ b (slot 4): Green=[O, M, b, Y]
Then test with SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: Red=[R, p, N], Green=[O, b, Y, M]
# Target (palette order): Red=[R, p, N], Green=[O, M, b, Y]
# Need to reorder green box: [O, b, Y, M] → [O, M, b, Y]
# Swap 1: b (green slot 2) ↔ M (green slot 4)
# Swap 2: Y (green slot 3) ↔ b (green slot 4 after swap 1)

action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 28},  # select b in green slot 2
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 40},  # swap with M in green slot 4
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 34},  # select Y in green slot 3
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 28},  # swap with b in green slot 2 (was slot 4)
    {'action': 'SPACE'}  # test
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
